Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for NorthWind-related communications using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "northwindtech"). Discover the full email history including: (a) Pam Kowalski's original request for 2 Denver relocations (Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz), (b) Victor Huang's formal security requirements email for Raj Patel's server lab chain-of-custody protocol, (c) Catalina's response to Pam acknowledging both moves and the Q1 service failures, (d) Catalina's response to Victor confirming she's working on the chain-of-custody requirements, (e) Pam's follow-up with Emilia's lease details (Lincoln Property Company, Greg Dawson, Chicago lease through May 15, $2,850/month, 60-day early termination clause already lapsed), (f) Victor Huang's escalation email to Chloe about the chain-of-custody breach - crew loaded before escort arrived and equipment was placed in overnight warehouse storage, (g) Craig Nguyen from KeyMove Specialty Transport's damage report on Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano (4.5-5 inch scratch on bass side rim during second-floor landing extraction), (h) Catalina's draft email to David admitting she's underwater on NorthWind, and (i) Pam's Q1 satisfaction survey response (5/10 overall, 4/10 communication, 5/10 service quality). Also, discover the SentinelChain Logistics dispatch confirmation for Raj Patel's supervised pickup (April 9, escort at 9:00 AM).
OE 2: Search Slack messages for NorthWind-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz" or "piano" or "chain-of-custody"). Discover: (a) Hana Kim's message in #finance (C005) about generating invoice INV-2026-0412 for $14,700 with the full breakdown, flagging the chain-of-custody line item ($1,200) as PROVISIONAL (priced from industry benchmarks since MoveOps has never offered this service), and noting that the hazmat surcharge ($1,800) and piano subcontractor line item ($2,200) are both based on preliminary vendor quotes that could shift once actual contracts are in place, (b) Catalina's message in #operations (C006) to Chloe about the 2 new NorthWind Denver relocations and their complications, (c) Chloe's detailed operational response about Swift handling hazmat but chain-of-custody being unprecedented for MoveOps, (d) Catalina's update in #customer-engagement (C002) about NorthWind being an account recovery opportunity after 5/10 CSAT, (e) David Chen's response in #customer-engagement offering enhanced SLA and staying CC'd on all NorthWind communications, (f) Julian Brooks' message in #customer-support (C007) that Emilia Cruz called angry about the scratched Steinway and wants a callback, (g) Blessing Okafor's message in #operations owning her part on the piano damage - she green-lit the extraction plan without requiring a pre-move site survey, (h) Marcus Thorne's message in #finance about reviewing the KeyMove bill for $1,200 insurance claim rider and NorthWind being "expensive and fragile," (i) Catalina's message in #operations saying she's drowning with six active NorthWind relocations, and (j) David Chen's message in #executive (C004) about Pam's direct escalation - final-warning territory, naming both the chain-of-custody breach and piano damage, and NorthWind having preliminary conversations with PathFinder.
OE 3: Search CRM for NorthWind Technologies using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind"). Find the company record (id: company_northwind, type: Customer, industry: Manufacturing Tech, 150 employees, Chicago, Catalina's account). Then search for NorthWind deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "NorthWind" or company_id: "company_northwind"). Discover FOUR deal records - this is the key CRM conflict the prompt references: (a) deal_northwind - $60,000, "NorthWind Technologies - 5 Enterprise Relocations", stage: proposalMade, no company_id, (b) deal_northwind_won - $60,000, "NorthWind Technologies - 5 Enterprise Relocations (Won)", stage: closedWon, no company_id, (c) deal_northwind_denver_expansion - $14,700, "NorthWind Technologies -- Denver Satellite Office Expansion (2 Relocations)", stage: qualifiedToBuy, company_id: company_northwind, (d) deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 - $14,700, same deal name, stage: contractSent, company_id: company_northwind. The Denver expansion has TWO conflicting deal records with different stages (qualifiedToBuy vs contractSent), and the original Q1 deal also has two records (proposalMade vs closedWon). This is the "conflicting deal records" the prompt describes.
OE 4: Search CRM for NorthWind contacts using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Pam Kowalski") and also search for other NorthWind contacts. Discover: (a) contact_pam_kowalski - pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, Director of HR Operations, (b) contact_raj_patel - raj.patel@northwindtech.com, Principal Architect, (c) contact_emilia_cruz - emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com, Director of Marketing, (d) contact_victor_huang - victor.huang@northwindtech.com, CISO. Also discover a DUPLICATE Victor Huang contact (crm_crmcontact_f10647847209) with a Denver address and no company_id, and a duplicate Emilia Cruz contact (crm_crmcontact_51d814273551) with Denver address, different job title ("Senior Product Marketing Manager" vs "Director of Marketing"), and no company_id. These duplicates are additional CRM data quality issues.
OE 5: Check CRM engagement history for NorthWind using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_northwind"]). Find the engagement eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context which provides the full account recovery context: NorthWind's Q1 issues (two Phase 1 apartments failed quality standards, slow response times, Pam escalated to David), the 5/10 satisfaction survey, and the Denver expansion as a second-chance opportunity. This engagement is written from Catalina's perspective and provides the account team with full recovery context.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for NorthWind invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria referencing "NorthWind" or customer ref). Find two invoices: (a) inv_northwind_01 (DocNumber: INV-2026-003) - $60,000 for 5 enterprise relocations, balance $60,000, and (b) inv_northwind_denver_02 (DocNumber: INV-2026-0412) - $14,700 for the Denver expansion with 6 line items: Standard Relocation Raj Patel $4,500, Hazmat Handling Surcharge $1,800, Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol $1,200 (PROVISIONAL), Standard Relocation Emilia Cruz $4,500, Specialty Piano Moving $2,200 (PROVISIONAL), Early Lease Termination Negotiation $500. The provisional items ($1,200 chain-of-custody and $2,200 piano) are the charges the prompt says Marcus cannot verify.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks for bills related to the piano damage claim using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria referencing "KeyMove" or "piano" or "NorthWind"). Find bill BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 from KeyMove Specialty Transport (DocNumber: KM-44192-ICR) for $1,200 - an insurance claim rider for the Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction. Due date: April 24, 2026. This confirms the damage claim has a corresponding entry in MoveOps' books (accounts payable), charged to "Claims & Remediation Expense."
OE 8: Search Airtable for NorthWind relocation records using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind Technologies") or airtable_list_records on the Relocations table. Find 8 NorthWind relocation records total: 5 completed Q1 relocations (Ryan Caldwell Detroit→Chicago, Lisa Petrosky Indianapolis→Chicago, Derek Nash Minneapolis→Chicago, Amira Okonkwo St. Louis→Chicago, Ben Schaefer Milwaukee→Chicago), 2 in-progress Denver moves (Raj Patel Chicago→Denver with status "In Progress" and Emilia Cruz Chicago→Denver with status "In Progress"), and 1 additional audit note record (recRajPatelAuditNote0411 - Raj Patel, April 10, documenting the escort timing breach: escort confirmed for 9:00 AM but movers arrived at 7:00 AM and loaded without waiting). Note that Raj Patel's primary Airtable record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) still shows "In Progress" even though the physical move was already executed - this is a data inconsistency flagged in Linear comments. Also find the NorthWind client account record (recAcct000000064) showing Catalina Dubois as Account Manager, 5 total relocations, and notes about timeline/quality issues.
OE 9: Search Linear for NorthWind-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel"). Find: (a) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind - "DOT Docs - Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS", state: Done, assigned to Chloe Vance, and (b) linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 - "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", priority: 1 (Urgent), assigned to David Chen, with description referencing Victor Huang's security breach email, Chloe's ops timeline, missing custody-log documentation, Julian's support note about Emilia Cruz, and Catalina's draft email about being overwhelmed. Read comments on the retention ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1") to find: Chloe's ops timeline reconstruction (crew arrived 7:00 AM on April 10, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, load proceeded before escort arrival, equipment held overnight at SentinelChain South San Francisco warehouse before onward release), a documentation integrity flag noting the Airtable record still shows "In Progress" and no signed custody log can be located, and Alejandro's finance draft for a possible 12% Q3 discount retention concession.
OE 10: Look up contact details for Hana Kim using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim"). Find hana.kim@moveops.com. Look up Alejandro Fuentes using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Alejandro Fuentes"). Find alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com. These are needed for the email to Hana and the Slack post for Alejandro.
OE 11: Clean up the CRM deal records for NorthWind. Update the Denver expansion deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage to set the correct, current stage on one authoritative deal record. The two conflicting Denver expansion deals (deal_northwind_denver_expansion at qualifiedToBuy and deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 at contractSent) need to be reconciled to a single accurate record. The correct stage should reflect the actual status - given that the invoice has been generated and services are being delivered, the deal should be at a stage like "contractSent" or "inprogress" rather than "qualifiedToBuy." The agent should update or note the discrepancy. Additionally, the original Q1 deal has two records (deal_northwind at proposalMade and deal_northwind_won at closedWon) - the proposalMade version is stale and should be noted.
OE 12: Log a CRM engagement note for NorthWind using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body containing the financial reconciliation findings, company_ids: ["company_northwind"]). The engagement should document: the invoice breakdown (INV-2026-0412, $14,700 total), which line items are provisional vs finalized, the chain-of-custody breach and its financial implications, the piano damage claim status ($1,200 KeyMove bill, insurance claim rider), the duplicate deal records found and which is authoritative, and the overall account risk context (Pam's final-warning escalation, PathFinder conversations). This gives the account team financial context when talking to Pam.
OE 13: Create a corrected or revised invoice reflecting actual costs. If the provisional charges have been finalized differently from estimates, use quickbooks_create_invoice to generate a corrected invoice for NorthWind Technologies. Based on the universe data, the chain-of-custody line item ($1,200) was flagged as provisional because MoveOps had never offered this service before and priced it from industry benchmarks. The piano moving line item ($2,200) was also provisional. The agent needs to determine whether actuals differ from estimates - the KeyMove bill shows $1,200 for the piano damage insurance claim rider (separate from the $2,200 moving charge), and the chain-of-custody service encountered a breach (crew loaded before escort, equipment stored overnight), which may affect whether the full $1,200 chain-of-custody charge is appropriate to bill. The agent should assess whether the invoice needs revision based on these findings.
OE 14: Post the full financial breakdown in the #finance Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005", payload containing the reconciliation details). The message should include: the NorthWind Denver invoice breakdown (INV-2026-0412, $14,700), status of each line item (which are provisional vs finalized), the piano damage claim ($1,200 KeyMove bill BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417), the chain-of-custody breach implications, the duplicate CRM deal records, the real margin impact, and any unresolved risk items. This is so Alejandro can validate the margin impact against his model - he's already been working on margin analysis and has a draft NorthWind retention concession model (12% Q3 discount).
OE 15: Send an email to Hana Kim using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: ["hana.kim@moveops.com"], subject referencing NorthWind reconciliation or invoice revision, content covering: which provisional invoice lines on INV-2026-0412 need revision now that actuals are available, the KeyMove damage claim bill (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417, $1,200), the chain-of-custody charge status, and what changed from the original invoice). Hana generated the original invoice and needs to know what changed.
OE 16: Communicate unresolved items and risk exposure in the final response to Marcus, and ensure they are also documented in the CRM engagement note (OE 12) and the #finance Slack post (OE 14). The agent should identify and flag: (a) the chain-of-custody breach - Victor Huang's escalation is unresolved, no signed custody log has been located, and the Airtable record for Raj Patel (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) still shows "In Progress" despite the move being executed, (b) the piano damage - Emilia Cruz's callback request was logged by Julian Brooks in #customer-support but no owner picked it up, the KeyMove $1,200 bill is in QuickBooks but the insurance resolution is unclear, (c) Pam Kowalski's final-warning escalation - she's named both incidents, missed the April 14 recovery-plan promise, and is talking to PathFinder, (d) the duplicate CRM contacts for Victor Huang (crm_crmcontact_f10647847209) and Emilia Cruz (crm_crmcontact_51d814273551) with no company_id linkage, and (e) the original Q1 deal record (deal_northwind) still showing proposalMade stage instead of closedWon.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for NorthWind-related communications using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "northwindtech"). Discover the full email history including: (a) Pam Kowalski's original request for 2 Denver relocations (Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz), (b) Victor Huang's formal security requirements email for Raj Patel's server lab chain-of-custody protocol, (c) Catalina's response to Pam acknowledging both moves and the Q1 service failures, (d) Catalina's response to Victor confirming she's working on the chain-of-custody requirements, (e) Pam's follow-up with Emilia's lease details (Lincoln Property Company, Greg Dawson, Chicago lease through May 15, $2,850/month, 60-day early termination clause already lapsed), (f) Victor Huang's escalation email to Chloe about the chain-of-custody breach - crew loaded before escort arrived and equipment was placed in overnight warehouse storage, (g) Craig Nguyen from KeyMove Specialty Transport's damage report on Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano (4.5-5 inch scratch on bass side rim during second-floor landing extraction), (h) Catalina's draft email to David admitting she's underwater on NorthWind, and (i) Pam's Q1 satisfaction survey response (5/10 overall, 4/10 communication, 5/10 service quality). Also, discover the SentinelChain Logistics dispatch confirmation for Raj Patel's supervised pickup (April 9, escort at 9:00 AM).
OE 2: Search Slack messages for NorthWind-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz" or "piano" or "chain-of-custody"). Discover: (a) Hana Kim's message in #finance (C005) about generating invoice INV-2026-0412 for $14,700 with the full breakdown, flagging the chain-of-custody line item ($1,200) as PROVISIONAL (priced from industry benchmarks since MoveOps has never offered this service), and noting that the hazmat surcharge ($1,800) and piano subcontractor line item ($2,200) are both based on preliminary vendor quotes that could shift once actual contracts are in place, (b) Catalina's message in #operations (C006) to Chloe about the 2 new NorthWind Denver relocations and their complications, (c) Chloe's detailed operational response about Swift handling hazmat but chain-of-custody being unprecedented for MoveOps, (d) Catalina's update in #customer-engagement (C002) about NorthWind being an account recovery opportunity after 5/10 CSAT, (e) David Chen's response in #customer-engagement offering enhanced SLA and staying CC'd on all NorthWind communications, (f) Julian Brooks' message in #customer-support (C007) that Emilia Cruz called angry about the scratched Steinway and wants a callback, (g) Blessing Okafor's message in #operations owning her part on the piano damage - she green-lit the extraction plan without requiring a pre-move site survey, (h) Marcus Thorne's message in #finance about reviewing the KeyMove bill for $1,200 insurance claim rider and NorthWind being "expensive and fragile," (i) Catalina's message in #operations saying she's drowning with six active NorthWind relocations, and (j) David Chen's message in #executive (C004) about Pam's direct escalation - final-warning territory, naming both the chain-of-custody breach and piano damage, and NorthWind having preliminary conversations with PathFinder.

Due date: April 24, 2026. This confirms the damage claim has a corresponding entry in MoveOps' books (accounts payable), charged to "Claims & Remediation Expense."
OE 8: Search Airtable for NorthWind relocation records using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind Technologies") or airtable_list_records on the Relocations table. Find 8 NorthWind relocation records total: 5 completed Q1 relocations (Ryan Caldwell Detroit→Chicago, Lisa Petrosky Indianapolis→Chicago, Derek Nash Minneapolis→Chicago, Amira Okonkwo St. Louis→Chicago, Ben Schaefer Milwaukee→Chicago), 2 in-progress Denver moves (Raj Patel Chicago→Denver with status "In Progress" and Emilia Cruz Chicago→Denver with status "In Progress"), and 1 additional audit note record (recRajPatelAuditNote0411 - Raj Patel, April 10, documenting the escort timing breach: escort confirmed for 9:00 AM but movers arrived at 7:00 AM and loaded without waiting). Note that Raj Patel's primary Airtable record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) still shows "In Progress" even though the physical move was already executed - this is a data inconsistency flagged in Linear comments. Also find the NorthWind client account record (recAcct000000064) showing Catalina Dubois as Account Manager, 5 total relocations, and notes about timeline/quality issues.
OE 9: Search Linear for NorthWind-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel"). Find: (a) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind - "DOT Docs - Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS", state: Done, assigned to Chloe Vance, and (b) linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 - "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", priority: 1 (Urgent), assigned to David Chen, with description referencing Victor Huang's security breach email, Chloe's ops timeline, missing custody-log documentation, Julian's support note about Emilia Cruz, and Catalina's draft email about being overwhelmed. Read comments on the retention ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1") to find: Chloe's ops timeline reconstruction (crew arrived 7:00 AM on April 10, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, load proceeded before escort arrival, equipment held overnight at SentinelChain South San Francisco warehouse before onward release), a documentation integrity flag noting the Airtable record still shows "In Progress" and no signed custody log can be located, and Alejandro's finance draft for a possible 12% Q3 discount retention concession.
OE 10: Look up contact details for Hana Kim using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim"). Find hana.kim@moveops.com. Look up Alejandro Fuentes using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Alejandro Fuentes"). Find alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com. These are needed for the email to Hana and the Slack post for Alejandro.
OE 11: Clean up the CRM deal records for NorthWind. Update the Denver expansion deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage to set the correct, current stage on one authoritative deal record. The two conflicting Denver expansion deals (deal_northwind_denver_expansion at qualifiedToBuy and deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 at contractSent) need to be reconciled to a single accurate record. The correct stage should reflect the actual status - given that the invoice has been generated and services are being delivered, the deal should be at a stage like "contractSent" or "inprogress" rather than "qualifiedToBuy." The agent should update or note the discrepancy. Additionally, the original Q1 deal has two records (deal_northwind at proposalMade and deal_northwind_won at closedWon) - the proposalMade version is stale and should be noted.
OE 12: Log a CRM engagement note for NorthWind using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body containing the financial reconciliation findings, company_ids: ["company_northwind"]). The engagement should document: the invoice breakdown (INV-2026-0412, $14,700 total), which line items are provisional vs finalized, the chain-of-custody breach and its financial implications, the piano damage claim status ($1,200 KeyMove bill, insurance claim rider), the duplicate deal records found and which is authoritative, and the overall account risk context (Pam's final-warning escalation, PathFinder conversations). This gives the account team financial context when talking to Pam.
OE 13: Create a corrected or revised invoice reflecting actual costs. If the provisional charges have been finalized differently from estimates, use quickbooks_create_invoice to generate a corrected invoice for NorthWind Technologies. Based on the universe data, the chain-of-custody line item ($1,200) was flagged as provisional because MoveOps had never offered this service before and priced it from industry benchmarks. The piano moving line item ($2,200) was also provisional. The agent needs to determine whether actuals differ from estimates - the KeyMove bill shows $1,200 for the piano damage insurance claim rider (separate from the $2,200 moving charge), and the chain-of-custody service encountered a breach (crew loaded before escort, equipment stored overnight), which may affect whether the full $1,200 chain-of-custody charge is appropriate to bill. The agent should assess whether the invoice needs revision based on these findings.
OE 14: Post the full financial breakdown in the #finance Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005", payload containing the reconciliation details). The message should include: the NorthWind Denver invoice breakdown (INV-2026-0412, $14,700), status of each line item (which are provisional vs finalized), the piano damage claim ($1,200 KeyMove bill BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417), the chain-of-custody breach implications, the duplicate CRM deal records, the real margin impact, and any unresolved risk items. This is so Alejandro can validate the margin impact against his model - he's already been working on margin analysis and has a draft NorthWind retention concession model (12% Q3 discount).
OE 15: Send an email to Hana Kim using send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: ["hana.kim@moveops.com"], subject referencing NorthWind reconciliation or invoice revision, content covering: which provisional invoice lines on INV-2026-0412 need revision now that actuals are available, the KeyMove damage claim bill (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417, $1,200), the chain-of-custody charge status, and what changed from the original invoice). Hana generated the original invoice and needs to know what changed.
OE 16: Communicate unresolved items and risk exposure in the final response to Marcus, and ensure they are also documented in the CRM engagement note (OE 12) and the #finance Slack post (OE 14). The agent should identify and flag: (a) the chain-of-custody breach - Victor Huang's escalation is unresolved, no signed custody log has been located, and the Airtable record for Raj Patel (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) still shows "In Progress" despite the move being executed, (b) the piano damage - Emilia Cruz's callback request was logged by Julian Brooks in #customer-support but no owner picked it up, the KeyMove $1,200 bill is in QuickBooks but the insurance resolution is unclear, (c) Pam Kowalski's final-warning escalation - she's named both incidents, missed the April 14 recovery-plan promise, and is talking to PathFinder, (d) the duplicate CRM contacts for Victor Huang (crm_crmcontact_f10647847209) and Emilia Cruz (crm_crmcontact_51d814273551) with no company_id linkage, and (e) the original Q1 deal record (deal_northwind) still showing proposalMade stage instead of closedWon.

tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

37 The model must search QuickBooks for bills related to the piano damage claim by passing a query related to \\"KeyMove\\" or \\"piano\\" or \\"NorthWind\\" or \\"damage\\" (or similar) when calling quickbooks_search_bills.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria VendorRef LIKE '%KeyMove%' and found the $1,200 insurance claim rider bill (KM-44192-ICR) for the piano damage claim.
✓Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria searching for VendorRef LIKE '%KeyMove%', which directly targets the KeyMove bill related to the piano damage claim. This search successfully found BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 for $1,200.
✓Run #3The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering for VendorRef LIKE '%KeyMove%', which returned the $1,200 Bill KM-44192-ICR for the Steinway insurance claim rider.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering VendorRef LIKE '%KeyMove%', which directly searches for bills related to the piano damage claim.
✓Run #5The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria [{"field": "VendorRef", "operator": "LIKE", "value": "%KeyMove%"}], which successfully returned the $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider bill BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417.
✓Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering VendorRef LIKE '%KeyMove%', directly searching for bills related to the piano damage claim vendor.
